summary: Re-tested frontend Match-column fix. Both iteration_4 bugs are FULLY RESOLVED. (1) OpportunitiesTable.jsx now renders 9 header cells aligned with 9 body cells — 'Match' header present right after 'Role / Company'. (2) OpportunitiesView.jsx now calls resumesApi.active() + matchesApi.batch() inside fetchOpps and listens for window 'resume-changed' event, so the Opportunities tab auto-refreshes after the user uploads/activates/deletes a resume in ProfileDialog. End-to-end verified against demo user: seed 5 opps → 5 match-badge-no-resume-<id> shown → upload Python/SQL/Pandas/Selenium/FastAPI .txt resume → ALL 5 badges auto-refresh to percentage badges (27%, 39%, 36%, 59%, 27%) with correct color hints (59% amber, others neutral). Popover for match-badge-<id> renders match-overall, match-missing, match-role-exp, match-exp-exp, match-loc-exp, match-tfidf-btn, match-ai-btn. Compute TF-IDF returned 2%. Get AI Insight returned AI score=45 + a real Claude summary in ~5s; reopening popover instantly shows the cached AI summary (no second LLM call). Freshness dropdown opens correctly showing Any time / Last 24 hours / Last 3 days / Last 7 days / Last 30 days. Phase 3A regression intact: outreach email dialog opens, history dialog opens, save-to-leads shows 'Saved to Leads' toast and the row appears in Leads tab, logout works.
critical_code_review_comments: Carry-over (low priority) from iteration_4: matches.py cache key omits profile.updated_at, so cached scores can be stale when preferred_roles/locations change. Consider invalidating match_results on PUT /profile.
Carry-over (low priority): resumes.py upload deletes ALL match_results for the user; could be scoped to previous active resume only for perf.
Carry-over (low priority): server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event hooks — migrate to FastAPI lifespan handler.
Carry-over (low priority): match AI + resume enrich routes parse JSON with greedy `\{.*\}` regex; strip ```json fences first to be safer.

success_rate.backend: 100% (47/47 from iteration_4 — not re-run as per request)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — all spec items verified including AI cache

seed_data_creation: Demo user re-seeded with 5 sample opportunities via POST /api/opportunities/seed. Uploaded a small .txt resume (Python, SQL, Pandas, Selenium, FastAPI, REST APIs, Git) under the demo user — left in place so the next agent can re-run with cached match results.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend Match-column fix is fully verified end-to-end against the live preview URL. Demo user currently has one active resume + 5 seeded opportunities + at least one cached AI insight (opp id 0cb52f88-bbc6-42f3-b062-110cd4eea64a). No outstanding frontend bugs from this scope; the only remaining items are the low-priority code-quality carry-overs from iteration_4.
rca of the issue: Both bugs from iteration_4 were confirmed fixed by direct UI behaviour: (a) table head now has 9 cells matching 9 body cells (verified by querySelector count); (b) badges go from match-badge-no-resume-<id> → match-badge-<id> immediately after resume upload without a manual reload, proving the 'resume-changed' window event listener wired in OpportunitiesView is correctly invalidating fetchOpps. No new RCA needed.
